text: Abstract: Purpose: This study aimed to investigate the extent to which the curriculum of the Bachelor degree program at a Moroccan university prepares graduates with skills that are required to achieve the 21st century skills leading to employability. Design/methodology/approach: A mixed-methods approach was used. The curriculum evaluation aimed at assessing the presence of some skills that are relevant to the 21st century such as creativity, innovative thinking, problem-solving and digital skills. The questionnaire was used to understand the students’ perception of their degree of readiness for work. The semi-structured interviews were used to investigate the teachers’ opinions regarding the degree of presence of the 21st century skills in the study plan and the degree of readiness of students for work. Findings: The results of the curriculum evaluation showed that most of the subjects were not aligned with 21st century skills. The results of the questionnaire showed that the majority of students are not ready to work. The results of the interviews showed that teachers believed that their subjects included the required skills, which led to students’ students’ lack of preparation for work because of their absence and tardiness. Originality/value: The study concluded that graduates from the Bachelor degree program need to receive more practical training through more stimulating teaching methods and to acquire metacognitive skills that enable them to develop self-motivation and personal responsibility leading to the development of a learning skill and adaptability and therefore ready to achieve the challenges of the 21st century. It also concluded the need for an immediate revision of the curriculum to meet the needs of the labor market required by the 21st century skills.
headline: Fully Human Written
